name: Chris Murphy (via DSCC.org)
subject: DeVos said something that stunned me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
I believe in gun-free school zones. I believe our children, our teachers, and their families should feel safe when they step on to school grounds. And while many people agree with me, it pains me to say that our President does not.
---
[](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecc4/2b07114c/44b9132e/736956bf/3868897710/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-devos?source=PT-20170206-Murphy-Pros")
Friend -- I believe in gun-free school zones. I believe our children, our teachers, and their families should feel safe when they step on to school grounds. And while many people agree with me, it pains me to say that our President does not.

So at the confirmation hearing for President Trump's nominee for Secretary of Education, Betsy DeVos -- I asked whether guns had any place in our nation's schools.

At first, she said, "I think that's best left to locales and states to decide."

So I pressed her again and asked, "You can't say definitively today that guns shouldn't be in schools?"

And then DeVos said something that stunned me.

To justify her position, she imagined the need for guns in Wyoming elementary schools, "to protect from potential grizzlies."

That idea would be laughable if it weren't so reprehensible.

**[So I need you to add your name to stand with all of us who are opposing this nomination.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecc4/2b07114c/44b9132e/736956bf/3868897710/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-devos?source=PT-20170206-Murphy-Pros")**

For weeks, all of us who serve in the Senate have been hearing constantly from voters about this confirmation, and it's having an effect.

Already two of my Republican colleagues have said they will vote against DeVos.

We need to continue to take every public action we can find to register our opposition before the final vote -- because right now, the vote count is tied 50-50. If nothing changes, Vice President Pence will get to break the stalemate.

**[So keep the pressure up -- sign on with the Senate Democrats to show that you're part of this fight.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecc4/2b07114c/44b9132e/736956bf/3868897710/VEsF/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-devos?source=PT-20170206-Murphy-Pros")**

Thank you,
Senator Chris Murphy

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee

Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,

name: John Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
I wanted to share with you some recent updates about the work I'm
doing to strengthen Ohio, champion our American freedoms and
share my views with a new book.

Peter, I wanted to share with
you some recent updates about the work I'm doing to strengthen
Ohio, champion our American freedoms and share my views with a
new book.

My Ohio Budget Proposal:

"Embracing the future" is the
fundamental theme of the new state budget I just sent to the
legislature, because it's the way I feel we can build for Ohio's
next generation.

As we've always done, our budget
starts with conservative, responsible budgeting that works to
restrain the growth of state spending and make government leaner,
more efficient and more effective. The budget focuses on what we
know works - tax reform, education, workforce preparation,
healthcare transformation and other areas critical to
long-lasting job growth - that have helped Ohio create 450,000
new private sector jobs over the past six years.

But this budget also emphasizes
forward-looking strategies that will keep our state competitive
for the jobs of the future, by ensuring that Ohio is a step ahead
with emerging technologies like self-driving vehicles, smart
highways, sensor technologies, drones, data analytics and
cybersecurity.

Book Announcement:

I've written a new book, that
will be released in April, to share what I've learned about
America while traveling all over our great country. I believe our
nation can be united again. The way forward is to focus on our
shared moral compass - the on that points us to honesty,
integrity, personal responsibility, faith, humility, compassion,
forgiveness and tolerance - that will get us back on track. You
can read more here from the Washington Post.

Recent Statements & National
Security Activities:

• I was very happy to see
the President nominate a conservative, principled and eminently
qualified pick for the Supreme Court. I'm hopeful that he will be
confirmed by the U.S. Senate.

• I also weighed-in on the
President's Executive Order regarding immigration. Here is my
statement:

• Also, I recently enjoyed
speaking on a panel about national security at the Netherlands
Embassy in Washington, D.C. I also visited with the German
Ambassador and voiced my strong support for upholding our NATO
alliance commitments.

• Finally, in mid-February,
I'll be traveling to Munich at the invitation of Sen. John McCain
to join a delegation at the Munich Security Conference, an annual
global forum to discuss security policy.

Thank you for your ongoing
support.

-John

John Kasich
Governor of Ohio

Paid for by Kasich
for America
www.JohnKasich.com

name: Glenn Davis
subject: please read
body:
Peter,
It's going to take bold leadership to restore the
economic opportunity Virginians used to enjoy when we were the
best state in the nation for business and job growth.

Yesterday I wrote an op-ed for one of Virginia's leading
conservative blogs, Bearing Drift, outlining the need for bold
actions, not just bold talk, and how Virginia can once again be
#1 for business and job growth. You can read the op-ed below.

Also, I invite you to my Facebook Live town hall
Wednesday night at 8 PM. My Facebook page is Glenn R. Davis.

(As published on BearingDrift.com)

We are halfway through the 2017 General Assembly session
and it's more clear than ever what Virginia needs. Virginia needs
bold leadership. Bold leaders have a history of bold actions, not
just bold talk. Bold leaders achieve what others have not,
because they are willing to do what others will not. Bold leaders
aren't bold sometimes, they're bold all the time; it's in their
DNA.

Bold is:

o Losing your job six weeks before Christmas and deciding
to start a company out of a one-bedroom apartment, turning it
into one of the nation's fastest growing IT companies a few years
later.

o Acquiring the assets of a publicly traded technology
company and taking it private after attempts by much larger
competitors had failed.

o Running for Virginia Beach City Council against a
24-year incumbent and wife of a democratic state delegate.

o Joining a team to create and grow a local public
charter school with an IB curriculum and deciding admission based
solely on a lottery system without deference to past grades.

o Pushing large tax reform initiatives as a freshman
delegate, earning the freshman legislator of the year award from
the Virginia Chamber of Commerce.

o Flying to MIT at one's own expense to examine new
technology around the use of coal in electronics, and to open
discussions for partnership with Virginia universities.

o Traveling to Estonia at one's own expense to create
opportunities for Virginia related to personal delivery drones.

o Creating an opportunity to ship Virginia coal to
Estonia to attract manufacturing opportunities around graphene.

I've done all this and more. My success is because while
most people just see the "black and white," I see the gray area;
that area where unique opportunities exist, but require more
effort and determination to achieve them.

Peter, you can finish reading the op-ed by following
the link to Bearing Drift.

Sincerely,

Delegate Glenn Davis

Authorized by DAVIS for LG

subject: Tell Congress: Don't legalize anti-LGBTQ discrimination
body:
Tell Congress: Don’t legalize anti-LGBTQ discrimination (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

The petition to Congress reads:
Block the First Amendment Defense Act, which would legalize anti-LGBTQ discrimination.

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems)

Donald Trump and Sen. Ted Cruz butted heads in the presidential primaries, but there’s one thing they can always agree on: discriminating against LGBTQ people under the guise of religious freedom. The First Amendment Defense Act (FADA), which Sen. Cruz is planning to introduce and Trump has said he will sign, codifies discrimination against LGBTQ people into federal law.1

Republicans are playing a dangerous game with the lives of LGBTQ people. FADA is likely to be just one of many attacks from the Trump-Pence administration. Recently, a White House insider leaked a draft of an anti-LGBTQ executive order. It shows we need to be preparing ourselves for a full-fledged federal attack on LGBTQ people and be ready to fight tooth and nail to protect our communities.2

We must show there’s not only massive opposition to FADA, but to Republicans’ entire extremist, bigoted and discriminatory agenda. Can you add your voice today?

Tell Congress: Hold the line on FADA and protect LGBTQ people from discrimination. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

FADA prohibits the government from holding any business or person accountable for discriminating against LGBTQ people. Specifically, it would allow people and businesses to discriminate based on the beliefs that marriage is between one man and one woman, and sex should only happen within such a marriage.3 To be absolutely clear, what Sen. Cruz and others who support FADA are saying is that anyone who has sex outside of a heterosexual marriage deserves to be discriminated against.

This legislation isn’t just dangerous for LGBTQ people. People, businesses and institutions would be able to legally discriminate against single mothers, unmarried couples and anyone who has sex outside of marriage with the blessing of the federal government. Those discriminated against would have no avenue for recourse, should FADA pass.

Tell Congress: Hold the line on FADA and protect LGBTQ people from discrimination. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

A majority of Americans support equal protection under the law for LGBTQ people, but that has not stopped attacks from conservative extremists across the country. In the majority of states, bigots can fire people from their jobs, evict them from their apartments or deny services because of their sexual orientation or gender identity and presentation.

In 2016, 100 pieces of anti-LGBTQ legislation moved in state legislatures across the country.4 From “bathroom bills,” which explicitly target transgender people by making it illegal for them to use the bathroom that aligns with their gender identity to Religious Freedom Restoration Acts, which make it legal for people to use religion as way to validate their discrimination against gay or transgender people, the attacks are varied in their approach but stem from the same bigotry and intolerance.5

Right-wing legislators are not only refusing to create a climate of safety and inclusion, they are also proactively attempting to dismantle the rights of LGBTQ people. Elected officials who stigmatize and target LGBTQ people contributes to a larger social context of intolerance. In a 2009 survey it was reported that more than half of LGBTQ people in the United States expect to be a victim of a hate crime.6

Right after the election, hundreds of thousands of CREDO activists signed our petition pushing back on the Russell Amendment in the National Defense Authorization Act, a provision which would have given religiously affiliated organizations license to discriminate against women, the LGBTQ community and religious minorities using taxpayer dollars, undermining the protections currently in place for people employed by federal contractors. We won that fight and showed the new administration that when they attack the LGBTQ community, they attack all of us.

Congress cannot allow right-wing, anti-LGBTQ Republicans to bulldoze LGBTQ rights. We need them to stand strong. Will you add your voice? Click the link below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/ (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

Thank you for all that you do,

Tessa Levine, Campaign Manager
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/FADA_dems/)

References:
1. Mary Emily O’Hara, “First Amendment Defense Act Would Be ‘Devastating’ for LGBTQ Americans (http://www.nbcnews.com/feature/nbc-out/first-amendment-defense-act-would-be-devastating-lgbtq-americans-n698416) ,” NBC News, Dec. 21, 2016.
2. Sarah Posner, “Leaked Draft of Trump’s Religious Freedom Order Reveals Sweeping Plans to Legalize Discrimination (https://www.thenation.com/article/leaked-draft-of-trumps-religious-freedom-order-reveals-sweeping-plans-to-legalize-discrimination/) ,” The Nation, Feb. 1, 2017.
3. O’Hara, “First Amendment Defense Act Would Be ‘Devastating’ for LGBTQ Americans. (http://www.nbcnews.com/feature/nbc-out/first-amendment-defense-act-would-be-devastating-lgbtq-americans-n698416)
4. Jennifer Bendery and Michelangelo Signorile, “Everything You Need To Know About The Wave Of 100+ Anti-LGBT Bills Pending In States (http://www.huffingtonpost.com/entry/lgbt-state-bills-discrimination_us_570ff4f2e4b0060ccda2a7a9) ,” Huffington Post, Apr. 15, 2016.
5. “Anti-LGBT Religious Exemption Legislation across the country (https://www.aclu.org/other/past-anti-lgbt-religious-exemption-legislation-across-country?redirect=anti-lgbt-religious-exemption-legislation-across-country) ,” ACLU, accessed June 23, 2016.
6. Michelle A. Marzullo and Alyn J. Libman, “Hate Crimes and Violence against Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender People (http://hrc-assets.s3-website-us-east-1.amazonaws.com//files/assets/resources/Hatecrimesandviolenceagainstlgbtpeople_2009.pdf) ,” Human Rights Campaign Foundation, May 2009.

Friend --

Today, Betsy DeVos' confirmation for Secretary of Education will come before the full Senate. The vote count is deadlocked, 50-50.

This fight isn't over yet. Senate Democrats debated on the floor all night to block DeVos for as long as possible, phones are still ringing off the hook, and letters are piling up -- all to protest how dangerous DeVos is to our children's education.

**[Will you stand with every Democratic Senator opposing this nomination?](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954eccc/2b07114c/448b5313/736950af/1136294867/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-devos?source=PT-20170207-Signature_DS-Pros")**

**Name:** Friend
****Last Petition Signed** : **N/A

name: Vice President Mike Pence
subject: A Vote for America's Future
body:
|

Friends,

Today, I cast the tie-breaking vote in the Senate to confirm President Donald Trump’s nominee for Secretary of Education: Betsy DeVos.

The vote the President asked me to cast wasn’t just for Betsy – it was also for America’s future.

Our nation’s success depends on the education of our students. In Betsy DeVos, we have one of America’s foremost advocates for educational opportunity and excellence.

For nearly three decades she has devoted her time, her talent, and her treasure to ensure that every child in America has the best shot at a better life. Countless students have benefitted from her efforts to promote an educational marketplace defined by innovation, opportunity, and real, meaningful choice.

The President and I agree that our children’s futures should not be determined by their zip code. Students should not be trapped in a system that puts the status quo ahead of a child’s success.

Betsy DeVos will have great impact as Secretary of Education. It doesn’t matter whether it’s a public school, a private school, a parochial school, a charter school, or any other kind of school – she will help ensure that every student has access to a good school.

We are grateful to Senate Majority Leader McConnell and all the Republican Senators who stood with us on this important vote.

The President is fully committed to this mission. Today’s vote was the first of its kind in our nation’s history, but the real history will be made through our unwavering dedication to America’s children – and to America’s future.

Thank you for all your help and support.

Sincerely,

Michael R. Pence
Vice President of the United States

name: BREAKING (via Van Hollen HQ)
subject: unprecedented vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
For the first time in history, the Vice President had to make the tie breaking | | **BREAKING: Betsy DeVos _barely_ confirmed as Secretary of Education with a 51-50 vote.**
---

For the first time in history, the Vice President had to make the tie breaking vote to confirm a cabinet nomination. This means a majority of Republican senators and Mike Pence gave Betsy DeVos a rubber stamp of approval, and blatantly ignored Americans nationwide.

Today's vote puts blind loyalty to Trump ahead of the American people. And it is unacceptable.

That's why Democrats held the floor from yesterday afternoon until the vote today to stand up for the millions of Americans who have been flooding Capitol Hill switchboards to speak out against the confirmation of Betsy DeVos.

[**We may have lost this vote, but will not back down. We will continue to fight for quality education for ALL students. Add your name to show your commitment to protecting our children 's future.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b32/70cdc2ba/47412b73/397ee0a/3145639135/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-on-commit-to-protecting-our-students-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+may+have+lost+this+vote+but+will+not+&utm_campaign=em020717&source=em020717")

The White House wants to claim that this is about obstructionist games, but that couldn't be further from the truth. This is about ensuring that taxpayers resources are not taken away from public schools and put into the hands of private and for-profit institutions that serve only a small number. It is about demanding that our goal as a country must be that ALL students have access to schools that are publicly accountable and focused on helping them succeed.

It's disturbingly apparent that the overwhelming majority of Republicans just didn't care that Betsy DeVos doesn't have the experience necessary to oversee our children's schools.

All but two Senate Republicans and Mike Pence have acted in direct opposition to parents and teachers across this country. But we will not back down. [**Add your name to send a clear message that we will continue to stand up for every kid 's education.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b32/70cdc2ba/47412b73/397ee0b/3145639135/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-on-commit-to-protecting-our-students-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+send+a+clear+message+th&utm_campaign=em020717&source=em020717")

Let's keep on fighting.

Team Van Hollen

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
